humanSummary.text: Final audit found no P0/P1 blockers for KAIFUU-014. Remaining redaction, profile write-gate, and secret-ref lint items are tracked as non-blocking DAG follow-ups.

findings.title: Report-wide redaction needs broader coverage
findings.description: Detection, profile validation, patch, golden report, and CLI error surfaces need a report-wide pass that scrubs or relativizes absolute local paths and raw diagnostic echoes.

evidence.detail: Final wave 11 audit recorded report-wide redaction hardening as a P2 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Host-specific paths or raw diagnostic echoes could leak from non-key report surfaces even though key material is already guarded.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Detection, profile validation, patch, golden report, and CLI error outputs do not expose absolute local paths from test fixtures or user inputs.
Diagnostics that echo adapter, helper, or validation payloads redact key material, private text, and machine-specific path fragments before serialization.
Golden report fixtures assert redacted or relative forms instead of host-specific paths.

acceptanceCriteria: Detection, profile validation, patch, golden report, and CLI error outputs do not expose absolute local paths from test fixtures or user inputs
Diagnostics that echo adapter, helper, or validation payloads redact key material, private text, and machine-specific path fragments before serialization
Golden report fixtures assert redacted or relative forms instead of host-specific paths
auditFocus: Absolute path leakage in structured reports
Raw diagnostic echoes bypassing report redaction
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node KAIFUU-043 for wave 11 completion bookkeeping.

findings.title: Legacy profile writes need the central gate
findings.description: The legacy `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` path must validate and redact profile writes through the same gate as profile init, or be removed.

evidence.detail: Final wave 11 audit recorded centralized profile write gating as a P2 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: A compatibility profile command can drift from the hardened profile-init path and reintroduce invalid or unredacted profile writes.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Every command that writes a key profile uses the shared validation and redaction gate before persisting output.
The legacy `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` path either delegates to the shared gate or is removed with a stable replacement error.
Regression tests prove invalid, unredacted, or raw-key profile payloads cannot be written through either command path.

acceptanceCriteria: Every command that writes a key profile uses the shared validation and redaction gate before persisting output
The legacy `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` path either delegates to the shared gate or is removed with a stable replacement error
Regression tests prove invalid, unredacted, or raw-key profile payloads cannot be written through either command path
auditFocus: Divergent redaction behavior between profile commands
Raw key material persisted through compatibility paths
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node KAIFUU-044 for wave 11 completion bookkeeping.

findings.title: Secret-ref lints need stricter edge cases
findings.description: Secret-ref linting should expand beyond current raw-key heuristics to cover unstable local ids, account or order ids, machine-specific names, and high-entropy edge cases.

evidence.detail: Final wave 11 audit recorded stricter secret-ref linting as a P3 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Secret references can avoid storing raw keys while still encoding user, account, order, machine, or high-entropy identifiers that should not become durable shared metadata.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Secret-ref ids that embed account ids, order ids, local usernames, machine names, or absolute path fragments produce stable lint diagnostics.
High-entropy secret-ref names beyond current raw-key heuristics are covered by fixtures with false-positive guard cases.
Lint diagnostics identify the unstable component without echoing sensitive values.

acceptanceCriteria: Secret-ref ids that embed account ids, order ids, local usernames, machine names, or absolute path fragments produce stable lint diagnostics
High-entropy secret-ref names beyond current raw-key heuristics are covered by fixtures with false-positive guard cases
Lint diagnostics identify the unstable component without echoing sensitive values
auditFocus: Secret refs that encode user or machine identity
High-entropy identifiers missed by raw-key checks
Lint diagnostics leaking the sensitive substring
